channel.title: Tri-County Driving Academy
channel.description: Professional driving and trade training in Virginia and Tennessee

item.title: About Us
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
About Tri-County Driving Academy

Tri-County Driving Academy/Skill Center was established in 1996 by D. Carter McGlothlin. The organization started out as a school for Class "A" CDL instruction. Over the years the Center has grown into a school that now includes training for Heavy Equipment Operators and Diesel Mechanics/Heavy Truck Maintenance. The school provides personal training to each student.

We are located at 1714 US Hwy 19, Hansonville, Virginia — between Lebanon and Abingdon, VA.

Our Instructors

Our instructors are certified in their fields and offer the needed "one on one" assistance to make sure that our students are ready to pursue their career before completing their training. All of our classes are fully equipped with up-to-date training materials to provide sufficient training in your area of interest.

Career Placement

We assist with job placement in all areas. With the growing need for qualified tractor trailer drivers, heavy equipment operators, and diesel mechanics, we can point you in the right direction to get you started in the industry.

  • Individual students receive both classroom and job-site training.
  • Students handle equipment in a variety of real-world situations.
  • Today's equipment complexity demands professional, well-trained operators.
  • Companies depend on us to supply that level of skilled, motivated employee.

Our programs are certified by the Commonwealth of Virginia and authorized by the Tennessee Higher Education Commission. Graduates may receive up to 12 college credits and a Career Studies Diploma from Southwest Virginia Community College or Mountain Empire Community College.

item.title: Tractor Trailer Training (CDL Class A)
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Tri-County Driving Academy's Commercial Driver / Class "A" CDL Training program will provide you with the knowledge and skills to become a Class "A" licensed driver qualifying you to drive long distances, regionally or for a local carrier. The occupational scale for a Commercial Driver averages $2,400 – $3,600 monthly.

Price includes cost of training, books, supplies, DOT medical physical, and drug screen.

What You Will Learn

  • Preparation for the Class-A Commercial Written Exams
  • Class-A Commercial Road Test
  • Pre-trip Inspection
  • Backing Maneuvers
  • Coupling and Uncoupling
  • Defensive Driving and Safety
  • DOT Physical included

Training Schedule

The training program is four weeks. The first week is preparation for the Class "A" commercial driver's learner permit. During class hours you will also be scheduled to complete your DOT physical (included in the price). The remaining three weeks are hands-on training in the trucks. DMV testing is completed during the last week of class.

Classes begin every three weeks — call for scheduling.

Job Placement

Tri-County Driving Academy helps place its graduates for employment with several major carriers. Many of our graduates complete employment applications and interviews before graduation, allowing them to go to work immediately after training.

Admission Requirements

  • Valid Driver's License
  • DMV driving record
  • Must pass a DOT physical

Upon completion, graduates may also receive 12 college credits and a Career Studies Diploma from Southwest Virginia Community College or Mountain Empire Community College. (College credits may not apply to all states — call 276-794-7660 for eligibility.)

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Classes begin every three weeks — call for scheduling

item.title: Heavy Equipment Training
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
The construction industry is a thriving business in constant need of competent employees. Companies are looking for safe and professional employees and Tri-County can help you become one of the best. The typical pay scale for Construction / Equipment Operators is $1,800–$3,600 monthly. Many graduates enjoy full benefits including major medical and retirement programs.

The Heavy Equipment Training program prepares students for entry level positions as operators with various construction companies.

Equipment & Skills Covered

  • Backhoe
  • Excavator
  • Dozer
  • Blueprint reading
  • Industrial safety concepts
  • OSHA training
  • Flagman certification
  • Forklift certification

Industry Credentials

Students successfully completing Heavy Equipment Operations Level One through Tri-County Driving Academy will earn industry-recognized credentials verifiable through the NCCER Automated National Registry.

Tuition includes tuition fee, books, supplies, and certifications. Call for more information.

Admission Requirements

  • Must be 18 years or older prior to the first day of class
  • Must be in sound physical health

item.title: Fiber Optics Training
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Fiber Optic design, installation and maintenance is a profession that will see increasing demands in the future. Our course provides the student with the knowledge and skills to become a Fiber Optic Assembler or Installer/Technician.

Job placement potential: Telephone companies, Internet companies, and Cable companies.

Program Details

Program Length: Three (3) weeks / 120 hours. 10 credit hours.

Exams Required: Certified Fiber Optic Technician (CFOT) Exam and classroom tests.

Schedule: Classes start on the first Monday of each month.

Topics Covered

  • Introduction to the basics of fiber optics
  • Guidelines for fiber optic design and installation
  • Techniques for pulling and preparing cable
  • Cable termination and testing
  • Splicing techniques
  • Safety practices

Admission Requirements

  • 18 years of age or older
  • Basic knowledge of reading and math skills

Financial aid may be available depending on locality of residence and household income. Call 276-794-7660 for details.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: $2,500 (all states) — includes tuition, books, supplies & tools
Classes start on the first Monday of each month

item.title: Diesel School
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
The need for diesel mechanics is increasing because the diesel engine is becoming standard in most of the nation's trucks, locomotives, and buses. They are also becoming more prevalent in light vehicles such as pickups and passenger vehicles. The Median hourly wages of Diesel Engine Specialists was $18.94 in 2008 (BLS), with the middle 50% earning between $15.25 and $23.58 an hour.

Our training will provide you with the knowledge and skills needed for an exciting and excellent paying career — with hands-on training and lab/classroom instruction providing a foundation in the latest diesel technology service and repair.

Curriculum

  • Diesel Engine Fundamentals
  • Hydro-mechanical Diesel Fuel Injection Systems
  • Computerized Truck and Bus Systems Management
  • Truck Electricity and Electronics
  • Truck Steering, Suspension, and Wheels
  • Truck Brakes
  • Truck Driveline Systems
  • Truck Heating, Ventilation, and Air Conditioning Systems

Upon completion, the student will receive a Diesel Mechanic/Heavy Truck Maintenance Certificate. Price includes tuition, books, supplies, and first testing fees for ASE Certification.

Financial Aid

Financial aid is available for Virginia residents. Please call our office at 276-794-7660 for more information about eligibility and application.

Admission Requirements

  • Must be 18 years of age or older

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: $21,500 (VA students) — includes tuition, books, supplies & ASE testing fees. Financial aid available.

item.title: Surface Mining Papers
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
The Surface Mining Papers course prepares students for Virginia surface mining certifications required to work in surface mining operations in the Commonwealth of Virginia.

Price includes cost of all training, materials, and books.

Please call our office at 276-794-7660 for more information and upcoming class dates.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Classes start once per month — call for dates

item.title: OSHA and Hazmat Training
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Our OSHA and Hazmat Training course prepares students to work safely in regulated industrial environments and to transport hazardous materials in compliance with DOT regulations.

Note: We teach all of the HAZMAT Endorsement content, however students are required to pay for the background check and endorsement fees at the DMV to get the HAZMAT endorsement added to their license.

Price includes cost of all training, materials, books, and supplies. Please call our office at 276-794-7660 for more information and upcoming class dates.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Classes start once per month — call for dates

item.title: Virginia Online Driver Improvement Course
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
This Driver Improvement Course is certified by the Commonwealth of Virginia and is acceptable at any Virginia Court that allows online driver improvement courses.

The course is available online and can be completed at your own pace.

To access the online course, visit: VA Online Driver Improvement Course

item.title: Driver Improvement Class
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Driver Improvement classes are held at Tri-County Driving Academy, Inc., 1714 US HWY 19, Lebanon, VA 24266. Call for our class schedule: (276) 794-7660.

Classes are held from 8:00am until 5:00pm.

This Driver Improvement Course is certified by the Commonwealth of Virginia and is acceptable at any Virginia Court that allows driver improvement courses.

item.title: NCCER Training
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Tri-County Driving Academy is now offering NCCER Training in Heavy Equipment Operations Level One.

Students successfully completing Heavy Equipment Operations Level One through Tri-County Driving Academy will earn industry-recognized credentials verifiable through the NCCER Automated National Registry.

This certification is recognized by employers across the construction industry nationwide. Call us at 276-794-7660 for more information.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Included with Heavy Equipment Training — call for details
Included in Heavy Equipment Training program

item.title: Welcome to the New Tri-County Driving Academy Website
item.dc:creator: admin
item.content:encoded:
Welcome to the new Tri-County Driving Academy website! We have updated our online presence to better serve our students and prospective applicants.

Browse our programs page to learn about our CDL, Heavy Equipment, Fiber Optics, Diesel, OSHA and Surface Mining training programs. For questions, please contact us or call 276-794-7660.
item.excerpt:encoded: Welcome to the updated Tri-County Driving Academy website. Browse our programs and contact us today to get your career started.
